Childhood Lead Poisoning Prevention Program (CLPPP)
The El Dorado County Childhood Lead Poisoning Prevention Program (CLPPP) conducts outreach and education about lead poisoning and provides nurse case management in the event of a lead-poisoned child.
Many children are exposed to lead through lead-based paint, lead dust and other contaminated objects. Lead poisoning can cause learning disabilities, and behavioral problems even at the lowest levels, and at very high levels, seizures, coma, and death. Children under age six are at greatest risk.
For Lead information or resources call (530) 573-3165 or visit:
CDPH Me and My Family Lead Prevention Resources
For child safety and recalled product info visit:
US Consumer Product Safety Commission Website
Lead Hazard(s) Complaints
Visit the
Code Enforcement Division - El Dorado County
to submit an online tip or complaint. Some local jurisdictions, such as South Lake Tahoe, have the authority to enforce regulations regarding substandard housing. To report a substandard housing condition in your rental unit, contact the City of South Lake Tahoe Housing Division at 530-542-7417. See the
CLPPB Statutes and Regulations
and
CA Health and Safety Code, Sections 105250-105257
for more information on substandard housing regulations.
